He is currently Founder/CEO of Vexor, an AI and Web3 identity startup based in Madrid, and was part of the Project Europe startup accelerator. llm_summary.executive_brief: Alen Tadevosyan is an Armenian national, born and raised in Yerevan, currently based in Madrid, ES. He holds a Bachelor's degree from IE University (Madrid/Segovia) on a full scholarship, previously worked as Chief of Staff at Revolut (Apr 2023–Jul 2024) and Global Strategy Manager in Spain, and is currently Founder/CEO of VexorAI (AI + Web3 Identity), active since September 2024. Debt details — amount, origin, age, and legal asset findings — are not recorded in the case file; call history and prior contact outcomes are also unknown. The address on file, Hermosilla 28, 28001 Madrid, is confirmed via Catastro as a 628 m² residential property (ref: 2054211VK4725C0042OU). The email alentad@gmail.com is active and confirmed on multiple platforms; a secondary professional email atadevosyan@arcanopartners.com appears in breach records. A risk flag notes that masked contact data from some platform checks does not match the lead email, indicating a possible email update on those platforms.
llm_summary.approach_context: ['Soccer and Formula 1 fan; described sports as a huge part of his life and identity.', 'Registered on KuCoin (crypto exchange), consistent with his AI + Web3 professional focus.', 'Registered on Deliveroo (food delivery platform), indicating Madrid-based daily activity.', 'Google Maps contributor: 7 reviews and 2 photos submitted.', 'Registered on Poshmark (secondhand retail platform).', 'Speaks English, Russian, Armenian, and Spanish.', 'Active on LinkedIn as alentadevosyan and logfoundation; Twitter/X @tadevosyan_alen registered to alentad@gmail.com.', 'Co-President, SOAR Madrid Chapter (Society for Orphaned Armenian Relief) — Armenian community affiliation in Madrid.', "IE University alumni featured in 'Your Own Path' series; active in Project Europe startup accelerator (Cohort 3)."]

breaches_details.details: Gravatar, an avatar service owned by Automattic, disclosed a 2020 incident in which an unauthorized party accessed user data, exposing email addresses and hashed passwords, with a portion of the email hashes later cracked.

Under Armour, an American sportswear and athletic apparel brand, was claimed as a victim by the Everest ransomware group in November 2025 in an attempted extortion alleging access to 343GB of data. Customer data from the incident was later published publicly in January 2026, exposing 73M email addresses. Many records also contained additional personal information such as names, dates of birth, genders, geographic locations and purchase information.

Data belonging to 2.6 million Duolingo users was scraped in January 2023. This data included real names, login names, email addresses, and internal DuoLingo service information. The leak stemmed from an exposed API that allowed threat actors to confirm email addresses associated with DuoLingo accounts.

Lumin PDF, a cloud-based PDF editing and sharing platform, suffered a data breach in 2019 that exposed the personal information of more than 15 million users. The stolen data included email addresses, passwords (stored as bcrypt hashes), and Google tokens, which could have potentially given unauthorized access to other Google services. The breach was believed to have been caused by a vulnerability in Lumin PDF's backend infrastructure.

Around 150 million users of the MyFitnessPal app experienced a data breach in 2018 that compromised their personal information. The breach involved unauthorized access to usernames, passwords, and email addresses. While Under Armour, the parent company of MyFitnessPal, quickly reset the passwords of affected users, it was later discovered that the old passwords were being sold on the dark web.

Adobe, the multinational software company, suffered a major data breach in October 2013 that initially seemed to impact nearly 3 million user accounts. However, the situation turned out to be far more severe than initially estimated, with the number of compromised accounts eventually ballooning to about 150 million. The breach occurred when attackers accessed user passwords and the source code for certain Adobe products, such as Cold Fusion. The compromised data, which included almost 150 million encrypted passwords, was subsequently found on a website frequented by cyber criminals. The Adobe data breach is considered one of the worst in history due to the massive number of records affected and the company's use of questionable encryption techniques.

modules.name: osint_web
modules.summary: Alen Tadevosyan is an Armenian national (born and raised in Yerevan) based in Madrid, Spain. He graduated with a Bachelor in Philosophy, Politics, Law and Economics from IE University (Madrid/Segovia campuses) and previously held the role of Global Strategy Manager at Revolut Spain. He is currently Founder/CEO of Vexor, an AI and Web3 identity startup based in Madrid, and was part of the Project Europe startup accelerator. He also served as Co-President of the SOAR (Society for Orphaned Armenian Relief) Madrid chapter, where earlier internship roles at Arcano Partners and the Central Bank of Armenia were publicly documented.

facts.claim: Born and raised in Yerevan, Armenia; Armenian national
Speaks English, Russian, Armenian, and Spanish
Recipient of full scholarship covering tuition for IE University studies ('Young Talented Leaders Scholarship Program')
Featured as IE University alumni in 'Your Own Path' series
